artifacts.content:
From: michael.peterson73@gmail.com
To: laura.peterson@email.com
Subject: Quick recap of today & Nathan update

Hey hon,

Just sitting in the parking lot at Grace for a minute before heading home from the deacon meeting and wanted to get some thoughts down while they’re still fresh.

I talked with Pastor Keller again about the parent support group. Hard to believe we’ve been meeting on the first Thursday of each month since January 2022 already. I still think back to Nathan’s panic attack at Novi High on 10/14/22 and those 72 hours at Corewell/Beaumont Farmington Hills – that whole weekend in the adolescent observation unit feels like a blur now, but it’s exactly why those 7:00–8:30 p.m. meetings in the church library matter. Tonight I added a new dad to the confidential contact list; I’ll update the password‑protected file later so I can follow up with him next week.

We also did a quick review of the benevolence fund with Mark Jensen. It reminded me of those months from March 2020 through May 2021 when I was tracking who had lost jobs, who was behind on DTE and Consumers Energy bills, and quietly working with Mark to send those $200–$600 checks. I’m grateful that particular season has eased up a bit.

On the home front: I told Nathan I’d drive him to trumpet sectionals Monday since you’ve got that late meeting at Novi Meadows. He said he might stay after to shoot around with a couple of the JV soccer guys, so I’ll pack my laptop and work on the Romans handout for Sunday’s 9:15 Bible class while I wait.

Let’s still try to protect Friday night for popcorn and a movie, even if we’re both asleep by 9:30. This week has felt long.

Heading home now. Text if you want me to grab milk.

Love,
Michael

metadata.subject: Quick recap of today & Nathan update

artifacts.content:
Title: Friday evening brain dump – work, church, family

Work – Midwest Tooling & Automation
- Need to finalize the fixture redesign for the GM supplier by Wednesday. Schedule a quick stand‑up with the shop guys Monday morning.
- Talk to HR about succession planning for my role – I don’t want to be the bottleneck forever.
- Reminder: call Mark (treasurer at Grace) during lunch about the last COVID benevolence spreadsheet from back in 2020–2021. Make sure all those rent and utility checks ($200–$600 range) are properly archived.

Church – Grace Lutheran, Novi
- Parent support group: email reminder Sunday afternoon for Thursday 7:00–8:30 p.m. in the church library. Mention confidentiality again, especially for new parents dealing with teen anxiety/depression like we did after Nathan’s episode at Novi High and the 72‑hour stay at Corewell/Beaumont Farmington Hills.
- Recovery fellowship history: I’m thankful for that season (Jan 2018–June 2019) meeting every other Tuesday at 7:30 p.m. in Classroom 3 with the 4–6 guys working through alcohol and prescription painkiller addiction, AA, OWI probation in Oakland County, etc. Not sure if that will restart, but keep their names in prayer.
- I still feel bad about how I handled the evangelism committee a few years back. I let things slide, missed several meetings, and dropped the ball on the follow‑up calls. If I take on anything new, I need to be realistic about time.

Family
- Call Emily in Chicago Sunday afternoon – ask about second‑year med school rotations.
- Text Jacob about spring interviews and whether he’s leaning Detroit or Chicago after MSU graduation.
- Ask Mom and Dad in Kentwood how the in‑home aide is working out post‑stroke.
- Block off Saturday morning for a 4‑mile loop at Lakeshore Park – cholesterol and sanity.

From: mpeterson@midwesttooling.com
To: pastor.keller@gracelutherannovi.org
Subject: Council / commitments

Pastor,

I’ve been meaning to write this for a while instead of just giving you half‑answers in the narthex between services.

The last couple of years have made me realize my limits. Between the deacon work (hospital and shut‑in visits, adult Bible class, the parent support group in the church library on the first Thursdays, and the occasional recovery fellowship check‑ins with the guys who used to meet in Classroom 3), plus aging‑parent trips to Kentwood and the kids being in three different life stages, I’ve been stretched thin.

That’s part of why I stepped off church council halfway through my first term. I honestly underestimated the time commitment – the prep for monthly meetings, email threads, budget discussions – and I was already feeling burned out from all the other responsibilities at Grace. I didn’t want to admit it at the time, but it was starting to make me resent Sunday mornings, and that scared me.

I’m grateful you trusted me with coordinating the COVID benevolence work back in March 2020–May 2021, tracking who had lost jobs or reduced hours and quietly working with Mark Jensen to get those $200–$600 checks out for DTE/Consumers and rent. I’m also grateful you invited me into the messier situations, like walking with Jim and Linda H. after Jim disclosed the affair during that elder visit on 9/10/19 and those four meetings in your office, plus the referral to the counselor in Livonia.

I don’t want to disappear from leadership, but I do want to be more intentional. Maybe the best use of my time right now is staying focused on the deacon work and Sunday Bible class, rather than adding more committees.

Happy to talk more in person after Vespers next Wednesday.

In Christ,
Michael

From: michael.peterson73@gmail.com
To: headelder@gracelutherannovi.org
Subject: Usher schedule mix‑ups (my fault)

Hi Dan,

I wanted to send a quick note and own the confusion with the ushers the last few weeks.

On both 1/12 and 1/19 we ended up scrambling at the 8:15 service because we didn’t have enough ushers in place. That’s on me. I sat down this afternoon and realized I had put together the new rotation but never actually sent the final schedule out or confirmed replacements when people emailed to say they’d be out of town. I’ve been juggling deacon visits, Nathan’s JV soccer and trumpet stuff, a couple of trips to Kentwood for my dad’s post‑stroke appointments, and Bible class prep, and I let the usher coordination slide.

Given everything else on my plate (parent support group first Thursdays 7:00–8:30 p.m. in the church library, the occasional recovery fellowship check‑ins from the old Tuesday 7:30 p.m. group in Classroom 3, plus the ongoing benevolence odds and ends left from the March 2020–May 2021 season), I probably shouldn’t have volunteered to take the usher schedule back in the first place. There were even a couple Sundays last fall where we were short because I forgot to follow up after someone texted me to swap.

I’ve attached an updated schedule through Easter and already emailed it to the usher list this time. If you’d prefer to hand this responsibility to someone a bit more detail‑oriented, I completely understand and won’t be offended. I’d rather admit my limitations than keep creating Sunday‑morning fire drills.

Thanks for your patience and for covering the gaps the last two weeks.

In Christ,
Michael

Event: Parent Support Group – Grace Lutheran (Library)
Date: 2025-03-06 (First Thursday of the month)
Time: 7:00–8:30 p.m.
Location: Church library, Grace Lutheran Church, Novi, MI
Attendees: 6–10 parents (confidential – do not list names here)

Description/Notes (for Michael’s eyes only):

Arrive by 6:40 p.m. to set up chairs in a circle in the library, make coffee, and put out tea and water. Double‑check that the door can be closed for privacy. Bring printed copies of the ground rules (confidentiality, no fixing, no shaming) and the resource list for local counselors and psychiatrists.

Opening (7:00–7:15)
- Quick prayer and check‑in. Acknowledge that this group grew out of our own experience after Nathan’s panic attack at Novi High School on 10/14/22 and his 72‑hour observation stay at Corewell Health/Beaumont Hospital in Farmington Hills.
- Remind everyone that this is a peer support group, not therapy.

Discussion (7:15–8:10)
- Topic: Navigating school systems when your teen has anxiety/depression.
- Ask if anyone has had recent issues with attendance offices, school counselors, or 504 plans.
- Be prepared to share how Pastor Keller encouraged me back in early 2022 to start meeting monthly (first Thursdays, 7:00–8:30 p.m.) after he saw how many families were quietly struggling.
- If new parents attend, mention other care ministries at Grace (benevolence support we offered during March 2020–May 2021 for families who lost jobs or had reduced hours; recovery fellowship that met every other Tuesday at 7:30 p.m. in Classroom 3 for men dealing with alcohol/painkiller addiction and OWI probation in Oakland County).

Closing (8:10–8:30)
- Closing prayer, confirm next month’s date, discreetly add any new contact info to the password‑protected list later at home.

From: michael.peterson73@gmail.com
To: emily.peterson@email.com
Subject: Quick family download before your exam week

Hi Em,

I know you’re heading into another round of exams at UIC, so I’ll keep this somewhere between “Dad novel” and “reasonable length.”

Things here are their usual mix of busy and good. Mom’s juggling her part‑time social work at Novi schools and a lot of heavy stories from kids. Nathan’s in the middle of JV soccer conditioning and band rehearsals. He’s doing better overall, but I still think about that panic episode at Novi High back on 10/14/22 and the 72‑hour observation at Corewell/Beaumont Farmington Hills. It’s part of why I keep the parent support group going at church on the first Thursday of each month from 7:00–8:30 p.m. in the library – there are more families like us than you’d think.

At Grace, I’m still wearing multiple hats: deacon visits, Sunday 9:15 adult Bible class, and some behind‑the‑scenes stuff. During COVID (March 2020 through May 2021) I quietly tracked which members had lost jobs or had their hours cut and worked with our treasurer, Mark Jensen, to send $200–$600 checks to cover DTE/Consumers Energy bills and back rent so people didn’t get hit with late fees or eviction threats. I’m grateful you kids never saw the worst of that.

Before that, from January 2018 to June 2019, I helped coordinate an informal recovery fellowship at Grace – every other Tuesday at 7:30 p.m. in Classroom 3 with 4–6 guys working through alcohol and prescription painkiller addiction and a couple on OWI probation in Oakland County. Different kind of medicine than what you’re studying, but still healing work in its own way.

Grandma and Grandpa in Kentwood are holding steady. Grandpa’s stroke recovery is slow but moving in the right direction. Mom and I are taking turns with I‑96 runs for appointments.

Proud of you. Text us when you’re done with your last exam so we can celebrate long‑distance.

Love,
Dad

metadata.subject: Quick family download before your exam week

artifacts.content:
Title: Private ministry log – Jim & Linda / reconciliation process

Date range: Fall 2019 – early 2020

Just want a concise record for myself, in case Pastor or the Board of Elders asks for a refresher later.

9/10/19 – Elder home visit with Jim and Linda H. at their place. During prayer time Jim broke down and disclosed an ongoing extramarital affair. We encouraged immediate honesty with Linda and set up a meeting with Pastor Keller.

Late Sept 2019 – First joint meeting in Pastor’s office at Grace Lutheran, Novi. I attended with Pastor. Lots of raw emotion. Agreed to confidentiality within a small circle (Pastor, me, elders) and to seek Christian marriage counseling.

Oct–Nov 2019 – Two more meetings in Pastor’s office. I handled scheduling, trying to work around my Midwest Tooling schedule and their kids’ activities. We referred them to a Christian marriage counselor in Livonia. I emailed them the contact info and encouraged them to commit to at least six sessions.

11/12/19 – Fourth joint meeting in Pastor’s office. I took detailed notes (stored securely) on what Jim and Linda both shared about trust, boundaries, and progress in counseling.

Early 2020 – Follow‑up phone call week of 1/6/20 to check how counseling in Livonia was going. Reported to the Board of Elders in summary form so they could pray and consider how to handle Jim’s leadership roles. We quietly stepped him back from visible positions for a season.

Reflection: This whole process, alongside coordinating COVID benevolence (March 2020–May 2021) and the recovery fellowship (Jan 2018–June 2019, every other Tuesday at 7:30 p.m. in Classroom 3 with 4–6 men dealing with alcohol/painkiller addiction and OWI probation in Oakland County), has shown me how messy and grace‑filled congregational care can be.

metadata.title: Private ministry log – Jim & Linda / reconciliation
